THERE WAS AN EF-1 TORNADO IN THE CONFINES OF DOLPHIN PLACE...POPE DRIVE...TO JUST THE NORTH OF MIRAMAR PLACE AND EAST OF STRAPLES ST IN THE CORPUS CHRISTI CITY LIMITS. SEVERAL HOMES WERE DAMAGED AND HAD SPORDIC DAMAGE IN THE POPE PLACE NEIGHBORHOOD. THE TORNADO HAS WIND SPEEDS OF 95 TO 105 MPH...WIDTH OF 50 TO 70 YARDS AND WAS ABOUT A QUARTER MILE IN LENGTH.

NWS STORM SURVEY INDICATED EF0 DAMAGE TO A HOME NEAR HWY 630 AND CR1747. THE ENTIRE EAST FACING SIDE OF A METAL ROOF OF A NEW HOME WAS BLOWN OFF AND TOSSED 50 TO 100 YARDS TO THE SOUTHEAST. THE TORNADO WAS ESTIMATED TO BE 25 YARDS WIDE AND TRAVELED 100 YARDS IN LENGTH. THERE WAS NO VISIBLE DAMAGE TO ANY OTHER SURROUNDING STRUCTURE.

EF0 TORNADO STARTING FROM COUNTRY CLUB ESTATES SUBDIVISION NEAR WOOLRIDGE AND STAPLES STREET. DAMAGE PATH 2.5 MILES LONG AND 100 YARDS WIDE AT ITS WIDEST. TORNADO LIFTED NEAR NILE ROAD AND SOUTH PADRE ISLAND DRIVE. LOTS OF FENCE DAMAGE AND LARGE TREE LIMBS DOWN IN PATH. MOST NOTABLE DAMAGE SARATOGA COVE APARTMENTS WITH TREEES, SHINGLES, ROOF AND FENCE DAMAGE. SHINGLE DAMAGE AT DELUXE EXTENDED STAY MOTEL. SIGNS ALSO DOWN IN AND NEAR PATH, INCLUDING FROST BANK.
FENCES WERE BLOWN DOWN ALONG STAPLES. FROST BANK SIGN WAS DAMAGED AT THE CORNER OF STAPLES AND SARATOGA.

PUBLIC REPORTED SIGHTNING A WATERSPOUT OVER CORPUS CHRISTI BAY. REPORTED BY KZTV ACTION 10 NEWS.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF-0 TORNADO OCCURRED JUST SOUTH OF THE INTERSECTION OF HWY 136 AND 188. AN RV TRAILER WAS BLOWN OVER AND LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE BROKEN ALONG A PATH 1/3 MILE LONG AND 20 YARDS WIDE. THIS TORNADO OCCURRED IN THE OUTER RAINBANDS ASSOCIATED WITH HURRICANE DOLLY.
CONFIRMED TORNADO TOUCH DOWN NEAR CR136 AND 1177. SEVERAL TREES WERE UP ROOTED. THREE SHEDS COLLAPSED. RV BLOWN ON SIDE. ROOF TOPPLED OVER. REPORTED BY KIII TV 3.
SHERIFF DEPUTIES CONFIRMED A TORNADO TOUCH DOWN INTO COTTON FIELD.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF-0 TORNADO OCCURRED JUST NORTH OF THE INTERSECTION OF U.S. 281 AND C.R. 433. SEVERAL TREES OBSERVED DAMAGED ALONG A 1/2 MILE PATH THAT WAS 50 YARDS WIDE. THIS TORNADO OCCURRED IN THE OUTER RAIN BANDS OF HURRICANE DOLLY.
FM 70 AT HIGHWAY 286 IN CHAPMAN RANCH IS BARRICADED DUE TO FLOODED ROADWAY.
EASTBOUND RIGHT LANE OF STATE HIGHWAY 44 NEAR CLARKWOOD ROAD IS CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING.
WATER IS ON THE SOUTHBOUND LANES OF WEBER ROAD FROM YORKTOWN TO OSO CREEK.
15 INTERSECTIONS IN THE CITY OF LAREDO CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING
FLOODING OF LOW LYING STREETS AND POOR DRAINAGE AREAS OCCURRING IN CENTRAL AND SOUTH LAREDO. REPORTS OF A FEW VEHICLES STALLED IN FLOODED AREAS.
SEVERAL CITY ROADWAYS AND STREETS WERE FLOODED.
THE LAREDO DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC SAFETY REPORTED A TORNADO ON THE GROUND WEST OF INTERSTATE 35 NEAR DOWNTOWN BY LAREDO COMMUNITY COLLEGE.
COCORAHS OBSERVER AND WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED THAT MCPHERSON ROAD WAS COMPLETELTY COVER WITH FLOWING WATER.

LAREDO P.D. REPORTED FLOODED ROADS AT TWO DIFFERENT INTERSECTIONS. THE FIRST INTERSECTION THAT WAS REPORTED FLOODED OCCURRED AT THE CORNER OF SAN GABRIEL DRIVE AND MINES ROAD. THE SECOND INTERSECTION THAT WAS REPORTED FLOODED OCCURRED AT THE CORNER OF ATLANTA DRIVE AND LOWRY ROAD.
LAREDO P.D. REPORTED FLOODING IN THE CITY OF LAREDO. THE INTERSECTIONS OF CLARK BLVD AND CEDAR AVE...MCPHERSON AVE AND JACAMAN RD...AND LAS CRUCES DR AND MINES RD HAVE BEEN CLOSED.
CITIZENS TRAVELING ON HWY 359 IN THE LAREDO CITY LIMITS REPORTED FLOODED ROADS.
CORPUS CHRISTI P.D. REPORTED STREET FLOODING AT KOSTORYZ RD AND HWY 358 SERVICE ROAD.
WATER SEVERAL INCHES DEEP FLOWING OVER SEVERAL ROADWAYS IN THE BEN BOLT AREA AND AREAS JUST NORTH.
1 WATER RESCUE PERFORMED DUE TO STALLED CAR IN FLOODED ROADWAYS AT CORNER OF SPRINFIELD AND MANN. OTHERWISE MODERATE STREET FLOODING REPORTED THROUGHOUT THE CITY.
LOCAL TELEVISION FOOTAGE CONFIRMED A SHORT LIVED WATERSPOUT OCCURRED ON OSO BAY.
